verbatim_text: So let me go on. Now, the holy grail of long context modeling is how to be flat in both patterns. This is important for scaling to longer context. And they're having many attempts, probably hundreds if not thousands of papers, you know, in this area. And let me pick the two most well-known ones. So one is called Mamba and another one called Gated Delta Net. You know, both have been deployed in frontier models at this stage. And you see that they have a better offset than sliding window over here. But really, the slope, the problem is scaling is the same. Now, let's think about how we might go about this problem. This is the same slide as I've shown before. And, you know, just to remind you, right, during training we didn't really have a lot of problems with a lot of data because we were doing gradient descent on the model. And this problem, this long context, really arises because we're trying to find the workarounds, which in turn is because the old rules of machine learning dictate that we can't change the model ways at test time. So, I mean, I guess the simple idea is, you know, what if we also do gradient descent on the model at test time?
